title: Repo git hook keeps Kage memory current after commits
summary: Kage should offer an explicit repo local git post commit hook command because Repowise has stronger auto sync ergonomics. The hook manager installs, checks, and uninstalls a marker delimited post commit block that preser
body:
Kage should offer an explicit repo-local git post-commit hook command because Repowise has stronger auto-sync ergonomics. The hook manager installs, checks, and uninstalls a marker-delimited post-commit block that preserves existing hook content, runs kage refresh and kage pr summarize in the background after commits, supports KAGE_SKIP_HOOK=1 and KAGE_BIN override, and can be removed cleanly.
Evidence: Implemented kage hook install/status/uninstall in mcp/kernel.ts and mcp/cli.ts with tests in mcp/kernel.test.ts; documented in README.md, mcp/README.md, and docs/guide.html.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp; node mcp/dist/cli.js hook status --project . --json; git diff --check
